purpose: Provides infrastructure as code (IaC) resources for Cross River Bank's Cos Lending Selling platform on AWS using Terragrunt and Terraform
keyFeatures: Aurora PostgreSQL database deployment and configuration
ECS Fargate tasks for web and admin APIs
S3 buckets for various data storage needs
Secret rotation functionality for database access

dataEntities.name: loan_funding
dataEntities.description: Table in the database that contains loan funding data, referenced in the secret rotation Lambda

infrastructurePatterns: Modular Terragrunt structure with centralized configuration
State isolation based on git branch for collaborative development
securityPatterns: Security groups for network access control

keyFiles.description: Main documentation explaining the repository structure, Terragrunt configuration, and development workflow
Centralized configuration with global variables used across all Terragrunt configurations
Aurora PostgreSQL database configuration template for all environments
AI API Lambda function configuration template
ECS Fargate configuration for the web API service
ECS Fargate configuration for the admin API service
SQS queue configuration for the selling-x component
Lambda function for rotating database credentials for AI API
Lambda function for rotating database credentials for external database access
Sample Airflow DAG showing environment information

findings.description: The repository configures database permissions for specific schemas including 'app', 'dbt_temp', and 'export', but their relationship and full data model is not clear from the infrastructure code

Different roles are granted different permissions (AI API gets access to 'app' and 'dbt_temp' schemas while external access gets access to 'export' schema) but the business reason for this separation is not documented
